verses.latin_vulgate: Parabolæ Salomonis, filii David, regis Israël,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭שְׁלֵי שְׁלֹמֹ֣ה בֶן־דָּוִ֑ד מֶ֝֗לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad sciendam sapientiam et disciplinam ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָדַ֣עַת חׇכְמָ֣ה וּמוּסָ֑ר לְ֝הָבִ֗ין אִמְרֵ֥י בִינָֽה׃
verses.king_james: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad intelligenda verba prudentiæ,  et suscipiendam eruditionem doctrinæ,  justitiam, et judicium, et æquitatem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֭קַחַת מוּסַ֣ר הַשְׂכֵּ֑ל צֶ֥דֶק וּ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט וּמֵשָׁרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut detur parvulis astutia,  adolescenti scientia et intellectus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָתֵ֣ת לִפְתָאיִ֣ם עׇרְמָ֑ה לְ֝נַ֗עַר דַּ֣עַת וּמְזִמָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiens sapiens, sapientior erit,  et intelligens gubernacula possidebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׁמַ֣ע חָ֭כָם וְי֣וֹסֶף לֶ֑קַח וְ֝נָב֗וֹן תַּחְבֻּל֥וֹת יִקְנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:

verses.latin_vulgate: Animadvertet parabolam et interpretationem,  verba sapientum et ænigmata eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְהָבִ֣ין מָ֭שָׁל וּמְלִיצָ֑ה דִּבְרֵ֥י חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים וְחִידֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Timor Domini principium sapientiæ ;  sapientiam atque doctrinam stulti despiciunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה רֵאשִׁ֣ית דָּ֑עַת חׇכְמָ֥ה וּ֝מוּסָ֗ר אֱוִילִ֥ים בָּֽזוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audi, fili mi, disciplinam patris tui,  et ne dimittas legem matris tuæ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ֭נִי מוּסַ֣ר אָבִ֑יךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝טֹּ֗שׁ תּוֹרַ֥ת אִמֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut addatur gratia capiti tuo,  et torques collo tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י ׀ לִוְיַ֤ת חֵ֓ן הֵ֬ם לְרֹאשֶׁ֑ךָ וַ֝עֲנָקִ֗ים לְגַרְגְּרֹתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fili mi, si te lactaverint peccatores,  ne acquiescas eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנִ֡י אִם־יְפַתּ֥וּךָ חַ֝טָּאִ֗ים אַל־תֹּבֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dixerint : Veni nobiscum, insidiemur sanguini ;  abscondamus tendiculas contra insontem frustra ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יֹאמְרוּ֮ לְכָ֢ה אִ֫תָּ֥נוּ נֶאֶרְבָ֥ה לְדָ֑ם נִצְפְּנָ֖ה לְנָקִ֣י חִנָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:

verses.latin_vulgate: deglutiamus eum sicut infernus viventem,  et integrum quasi descendentem in lacum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִ֭בְלָעֵם כִּשְׁא֣וֹל חַיִּ֑ים וּ֝תְמִימִ֗ים כְּי֣וֹרְדֵי בֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:

verses.latin_vulgate: omnem pretiosam substantiam reperiemus ;  implebimus domos nostras spoliis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־ה֣וֹן יָקָ֣ר נִמְצָ֑א נְמַלֵּ֖א בָתֵּ֣ינוּ שָׁלָֽל׃
verses.king_james: We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil:

verses.latin_vulgate: sortem mitte nobiscum,  marsupium unum sit omnium nostrum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גּ֭וֹרָ֣לְךָ תַּפִּ֣יל בְּתוֹכֵ֑נוּ כִּ֥יס אֶ֝חָ֗ד יִהְיֶ֥ה לְכֻלָּֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse:

verses.latin_vulgate: fili mi, ne ambules cum eis ;  prohibe pedem tuum a semitis eorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנִ֗י אַל־תֵּלֵ֣ךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ אִתָּ֑ם מְנַ֥ע רַ֝גְלְךָ֗ מִנְּתִיבָתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:

verses.latin_vulgate: pedes enim illorum ad malum currunt,  et festinant ut effundant sanguinem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י רַ֭גְלֵיהֶם לָרַ֣ע יָר֑וּצוּ וִ֝ימַהֲר֗וּ לִשְׁפׇּךְ־דָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Frustra autem jacitur rete  ante oculos pennatorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־חִ֭נָּם מְזֹרָ֣ה הָרָ֑שֶׁת בְּ֝עֵינֵ֗י כׇּל־בַּ֥עַל כָּנָֽף׃
verses.king_james: Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi quoque contra sanguinem suum insidiantur,  et moliuntur fraudes contra animas suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭הֵם לְדָמָ֣ם יֶאֱרֹ֑בוּ יִ֝צְפְּנ֗וּ לְנַפְשֹׁתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic semitæ omnis avari :  animas possidentium rapiunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֗ן אׇ֭רְחוֹת כׇּל־בֹּ֣צֵֽעַ בָּ֑צַע אֶת־נֶ֖פֶשׁ בְּעָלָ֣יו יִקָּֽח׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; which taketh away the life of the owners thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia foris prædicat ;  in plateis dat vocem suam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇ֭כְמוֹת בַּח֣וּץ תָּרֹ֑נָּה בָּ֝רְחֹב֗וֹת תִּתֵּ֥ן קוֹלָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:

verses.latin_vulgate: in capite turbarum clamitat ;  in foribus portarum urbis profert verba sua, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרֹ֥אשׁ הֹמִיּ֗וֹת תִּ֫קְרָ֥א בְּפִתְחֵ֖י שְׁעָרִ֥ים בָּעִ֗יר אֲמָרֶ֥יהָ תֹאמֵֽר׃
verses.king_james: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Usquequo, parvuli, diligitis infantiam,  et stulti ea quæ sibi sunt noxia cupient,  et imprudentes odibunt scientiam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־מָתַ֣י ׀ פְּתָיִם֮ תְּֽאֵהֲב֫וּ־פֶ֥תִי וְלֵצִ֗ים לָ֭צוֹן חָמְד֣וּ לָהֶ֑ם וּ֝כְסִילִ֗ים יִשְׂנְאוּ־דָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?

verses.latin_vulgate: convertimini ad correptionem meam.  En proferam vobis spiritum meum,  et ostendam vobis verba mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תָּשׁ֗וּבוּ לְֽת֫וֹכַחְתִּ֥י הִנֵּ֤ה אַבִּ֣יעָה לָכֶ֣ם רוּחִ֑י אוֹדִ֖יעָה דְבָרַ֣י אֶתְכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia vocavi, et renuistis ;  extendi manum meam, et non fuit qui aspiceret :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַ֣עַן קָ֭רָאתִי וַתְּמָאֵ֑נוּ נָטִ֥יתִי יָ֝דִ֗י וְאֵ֣ין מַקְשִֽׁיב׃
verses.king_james: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;

verses.latin_vulgate: despexistis omne consilium meum,  et increpationes meas neglexistis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּפְרְע֥וּ כׇל־עֲצָתִ֑י וְ֝תוֹכַחְתִּ֗י לֹ֣א אֲבִיתֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego quoque in interitu vestro ridebo,  et subsannabo cum vobis id quod timebatis advenerit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־אֲ֭נִי בְּאֵידְכֶ֣ם אֶשְׂחָ֑ק אֶ֝לְעַ֗ג בְּבֹ֣א פַחְדְּכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum irruerit repentina calamitas,  et interitus quasi tempestas ingruerit ;  quando venerit super vos tribulatio et angustia :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּבֹ֤א (כשאוה) [כְשׁוֹאָ֨ה ׀] פַּחְדְּכֶ֗ם וְֽ֭אֵידְכֶם כְּסוּפָ֣ה יֶאֱתֶ֑ה בְּבֹ֥א עֲ֝לֵיכֶ֗ם צָרָ֥ה וְצוּקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc invocabunt me, et non exaudiam ;  mane consurgent, et non invenient me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ז יִ֭קְרָאֻנְנִי וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה יְ֝שַׁחֲרֻ֗נְנִי וְלֹ֣א יִמְצָאֻֽנְנִי׃
verses.king_james: Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:

verses.latin_vulgate: eo quod exosam habuerint disciplinam,  et timorem Domini non susceperint,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֭חַת כִּֽי־שָׂ֣נְאוּ דָ֑עַת וְיִרְאַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה לֹ֣א בָחָֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the Lord:

verses.latin_vulgate: nec acquieverint consilio meo,  et detraxerint universæ correptioni meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־אָב֥וּ לַעֲצָתִ֑י נָ֝אֲצ֗וּ כׇּל־תּוֹכַחְתִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comedent igitur fructus viæ suæ,  suisque consiliis saturabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭יֹאכְלוּ מִפְּרִ֣י דַרְכָּ֑ם וּֽמִמֹּעֲצֹ֖תֵיהֶ֣ם יִשְׂבָּֽעוּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aversio parvulorum interficiet eos,  et prosperitas stultorum perdet illos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י מְשׁוּבַ֣ת פְּתָיִ֣ם תַּהַרְגֵ֑ם וְשַׁלְוַ֖ת כְּסִילִ֣ים תְּאַבְּדֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem me audierit, absque terrore requiescet,  et abundantia perfruetur, timore malorum sublato.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשֹׁמֵ֣עַֽ לִ֭י יִשְׁכׇּן־בֶּ֑טַח וְ֝שַׁאֲנַ֗ן מִפַּ֥חַד רָעָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil.
